news.title: New Zealand hits study destination target a decade early
news.summary_en: New Zealand has hit a key international education target nearly a decade ahead of schedule, according to a new survey by Education New Zealand Manapou ki te Ao (ENZ).
news.author: Charlotte Greenstein

news.title: China’s outbound student drop signals “maturing” market
news.summary_en: The return of China’s outbound student mobility rates to 2016 levels signals a market “reset” rather than collapse, say experts, as Beijing releases outgoing student mobility data for the first time in five years.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Visa-refused students owed €60k by rogue Irish ELT schools
news.summary_en: Several English language schools in Ireland are under scrutiny over their failure to refund prospective international students whose visa applications have been refused.
news.author: Charlotte Greenstein

news.title: IDP expands into Malaysia amid shifting student preferences
news.summary_en: The international education provider, long focused on major Anglophone destinations such as the US, UK, Australia and Canada, is now turning to Malaysia as it emerges as a go-to option for degree-seeking students across Asia.
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: India’s TNE push is exposing a workforce challenge universities are not addressing
news.summary_en: As India experiences a transnational education (TNE) boom, Annette Ord explores the very real practicalities that institutions often overlook when opening overseas branch campuses.
news.author: Annette Ord

news.title: What’s in Canada’s CIMM report and will it influence IRCC policy?
news.summary_en: Canada’s Standing Committee on Citizenship and Immigration (CIMM) has unveiled a sweeping set of recommendations on the International Student Program (ISP). But how much will they influence IRCC policy?
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Malvern St James under threat of closure as financial pressures mount
news.summary_en: UK K-12 boarding school Malvern St James has announced proposals to close at the end of the academic year, against a backdrop of increasing costs and a tax imposed on independent school fees.
news.author: Beth Kennedy
